metadata.summary: Toolbox for manipulating the JSON-Format used by Synth Riders Beatmap Editor in the clipboard

documentation:
# Synth Mapping Helper

Toolbox for manipulating the JSON-Format used by *Synth Riders Beatmap Editor* in the clipboard.

## Features

For more info on each feature, check out [the wiki](https://github.com/adosikas/synth_mapping_helper/wiki) 

* Filter by note and wall types for all operations
* Changing type of notes and walls
  * Can cycle between multiple colors to make "rainbows" or alternate between single hand specials and regular notes
* Change BPM indepenent of note timing (helpful if you have a map with different BPM sections)
* Movement in XY and Time:  
    Note: All operation can be done in regards to grid center, a pivot point, or the start of rails
    * Rotate
    * Scale and mirror
    * Outset (moving outwards/away by a fixed distance)
    * Offset (Translate/Move)
    * Stacking patterns
* Pattern generation
    * Spirals/Zigzags
    * Spike/Buzz-Rails
    * Stack along rails
* Rail manipulation
    * Merging
    * Splitting
    * Interpolation
    * Convert between single notes and rails
    * Snapping single notes to rails
* Cross-Platform (Windows, Linux)
    * For Windows, supports drag and drop actions (fully usable without command prompt)
* Imports directly from clipboard, and export to it
* Uses an internal format that is easy to work with ([wiki page](https://github.com/adosikas/synth_mapping_helper/wiki/Glossary#measurement-system)):
    * Position in editor grid coordinates (+x=right, +y=up)
    * Time in measures (starting from start of selection)
    * Angles in degrees (positive=counterclockwise)
    * Notes/Rails seperated by color and as `n x 3` numpy-arrays (x, y, time)
    * Walls as `1 x 5` numpy-arrays (x, y, time, type, rotation)
    * Walls positions are adjusted to match their rotation center
* [Companion application](https://github.com/adosikas/synth_mapping_helper/wiki/Companion):
    * **Automatic backups** while mapping
    * Plot notes to spot outliers
    * View hand velocity and acceleration to find sections to smooth out
    * Show wall density and estimatation which ones will not render on quest
    * Fix **wall offset** between editor and game ("Finalize")

### Maybe (contributions welcome)
* GUI for common operations
* Automatic smoothing

## How to Install and use

### Advanced users
* Install via `pip3 install synth_mapping_helper` (requires Python 3.9 or higher)
* See `python3 -m synth_mapping_helper.cli -h` for usage of the clipboard manipulation
* See `python3 -m synth_mapping_helper.companion -h` for usage of the companion
* Feel free to experiment with extending functionality by using the module functions directly. If you have something that you think could help other mappers, please make a PR

### Windows

See [this wiki page](https://github.com/adosikas/synth_mapping_helper/wiki/Installation-and-Usage-on-Windows) for detailed instructions, including screenshots.

methods.name: synth_mapping_helper.utils.SecondFloat.__init__
documentation: dummy class to hold numbers parsed as seconds until bpm was parsed

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.finalize.RENDER_WINDOW
documentation: 4 seconds of elements are rendered

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.finalize.WALL_DESPAWN_PC
documentation: limit of visible walls, earlier ones despawn

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.split_rails
documentation: splits rails at single notes
Note: None of these functions are allowed to *modify* the input dict instance. Returning the same dict (if nothing needed to be changed) is allowed.

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.snap_singles_to_rail
documentation: snap single notes to rail

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.merge_sequential_rails
documentation: merges rails where end and start a very close

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.merge_rails
documentation: merges rails

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.connect_singles
documentation: Turn single notes into rails

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.interpolate_nodes
documentation: places nodes at defined interval along the rail, interpolating between existing nodes. Can be negative to start from end.

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.rails_to_singles
documentation: Turn all rails into single notes

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.rails_to_notestacks
documentation: Turn all rails into notestacks. Can be negative to start from wnd

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.shorten_rail
documentation: Cut a bit of the end or start (if negative) of the rail

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.extend_level
documentation: Add a level rail section at the end or start (if negative). Turns single notes into rails

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.extend_straight
documentation: Add a straight rail section at the end or start (if negative) which keeps the same direction of the previous segment

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.extend_to_next
documentation: Add a rail section at the end pointing to next note/rail. Vice-versa for negative. Turns single notes into rails.

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.rails.segment_rail
documentation: Segment rails into multiple range of maximum length. Can be negative to segment from end

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.RENDER_WINDOW
documentation: 4 seconds of elements are rendered

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.WALL_DESPAWN_PC
documentation: limit of visible walls, earlier ones despawn

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.QUEST_WALL_DELAY
documentation: 50 ms on highest wall density

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.DEFAULT_OUTPUT_FILE
documentation: inside working directory

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.DEFAULT_BACKUP_DIR
documentation: inside working directory

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.DEFAULT_VELOCITY_WINDOW
documentation: half a second gap breaks up velocity / acceleration calculations

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.AUTORELOAD_WAIT_SEC

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.AUTORELOAD_COOLDOWN_SEC

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.companion.AUTOBACKUP_DEFAULT_MIN

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.angle_to_xy
documentation: convert a angles in degrees to x and y coordinates
generic helpers

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.random_ring
documentation: random positons along a ring of radius 1

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.random_xy
documentation: random positions in a rectangle

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.spiral
documentation: spiral with radius 1, uses random when fidelity is 0
pattern generation

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.spikes
documentation: spikes with radius 1, uses random when fidelity is 0

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.create_parallel
documentation:
create parallel patterns by splitting specials, or adding the other hand

when splitting specials, both will be moved by half the distance left and right.
otherwise, the existing color stays, while the new hand is moved by distance in the "natural" direction.

distance can be negative to create crossovers.

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.find_wall_patterns
documentation: try to find repeating "patterns" with identical wall type and timing, returns wall count per pattern, pattern count and pattern length for all possible candidates

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.blend_wall_single
documentation:
create blending substeps between two patterns

first and second must be numpy arrays of the patterns (each with n walls)
with_symmetry can be set to false to disregard that symetrical walls (like squares) look the same in multiple orientations

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.pattern_generation.blend_walls_multiple
documentation: calls blend_wall_single to blending between multiple patterns, each with n walls

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.gui_tabs.utils.handle_errors
documentation: decorator for pretty error handling

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.gui_tabs.utils.read_clipboard
documentation: wrappers, for when I decide to use the browser clipboard

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.gui_tabs.utils.safe_clipboard_data

functions.name: synth_mapping_helper.cli._movement_helper

modules.name: synth_mapping_helper.gui_tabs.local_dir_picker
documentation: based on: https://github.com/zauberzeug/nicegui/blob/main/examples/local_file_picker/local_file_picker.py

methods.name: synth_mapping_helper.gui_tabs.local_dir_picker.local_dir_picker.__init__
documentation:
Local File Picker

This is a simple file picker that allows you to select a file from the local filesystem where NiceGUI is running.

```
param directory

The directory to start in.

param upper_limit

The directory to stop at (None: no limit, default: same as the starting directory).
```

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.RAIL_NODE_DIST
documentation: warn for long rails without intermediate nodes

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.HEAD_RADIUS_SQ
documentation: pre-squared, such that we can skip the sqrt in sqrt(x**2+y**2) < HEAD_RADIUS

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.SPIRAL_APEX
documentation: 65 cm away from the center the notes stop getting further out

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.SPIRAL_FLIP
documentation: 80 cm away from the center the math breaks down completely and returns to center, then flips to other side

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.CURVE_WINDOW_S
documentation: time between notes for break in curve

variables.name: synth_mapping_helper.analysis.CURVE_INTERP
documentation: 192 bins per beat

documentation: rails have a lower weight than notes
